title: lora_l2.c + lichen_l2.c: error code space collision between modules
description:
Cross-file interaction issue: lora_l2.c uses standard errno (EINVAL, EAGAIN, ECANCELED, etc.) while lichen_l2.c passes through both standard errno AND LICHEN-specific codes (LICHEN_EAUTH=200).

lora_l2.c:602-617 returns -EINVAL, -ECANCELED, -EBUSY, etc.
lichen_l2.c:540,580,606 uses -LICHEN_EAUTH (value 200)

When lichen_l2_send() at line 956 calls lichen_link_tx() and it fails:
  ret = lichen_link_tx(&link_ctx, ...);
  if (ret < 0) {
    LOG_ERR('... frame build failed: %s (%d)', lichen_link_strerror(ret), ret);
    return ret;  // Returns LICHEN-specific error to Zephyr

Zephyr's net_l2 send callback returns int, but Zephyr only expects standard errno values. LICHEN_EAUTH (200) may confuse Zephyr's error handling.

Similarly lichen_l2_input() at line 1869 logs lichen_link_strerror(ret) but returns void.

FIX: Either translate LICHEN errors to standard errno at module boundary, or document that LICHEN_EAUTH can be returned from L2 send. Safest is to map LICHEN_EAUTH to -EPERM or -EACCES at the lichen_l2.c boundary.
close_reason: Mapped LICHEN-specific link errors to standard errno before returning from the Zephyr L2 send callback; verified lichen_l2.c object rebuild and configured build
